name: Multi-Stage Docker Build for Go Binaries
description: Create a minimal Docker image for Go applications using multi-stage builds with scratch or distroless base images.

steps.title: Create a multi-stage Dockerfile
steps.description: Write a Dockerfile that compiles the Go binary in one stage and copies it to a minimal runtime image.
steps.command:
cat > Dockerfile << 'EOF'
# Build stage
FROM golang:1.23-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download
COPY . .
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o /app/bin/server ./cmd/myapp

# Runtime stage
FROM gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12
COPY --from=builder /app/bin/server /server
EXPOSE 8080
ENTRYPOINT ["/server"]
EOF
steps.tip: Using distroless gives you a minimal image with no shell or package manager, reducing attack surface.

steps.title: Add a .dockerignore file
steps.description: Exclude unnecessary files from the Docker build context for faster builds.

steps.title: Build the Docker image
steps.description: Build the image and tag it with a version.

steps.title: Check the image size
steps.description: Verify the final image is small, typically under 20MB for a Go binary.
steps.tip: Distroless images typically produce 10-20MB images. Using scratch gives even smaller images but has no CA certificates or timezone data.

steps.title: Run and test the container
steps.description: Start the container and verify the application is working.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Go Multi-Stage Docker Build
set -euo pipefail

APP=${1:-"myapp"}
TAG=${2:-"latest"}

echo "=== Building Docker Image ==="
docker build -t "$APP:$TAG" .

echo ""
echo "=== Image Size ==="
docker images "$APP" --format "table {{.Repository}}	{{.Tag}}	{{.Size}}"

echo ""
echo "=== Docker Commands ==="
echo "Build:   docker build -t $APP:$TAG ."
echo "Run:     docker run --rm -p 8080:8080 $APP:$TAG"
echo "Shell:   docker run --rm -it --entrypoint sh $APP:$TAG"
echo "Push:    docker push $APP:$TAG"
echo "Inspect: docker inspect $APP:$TAG"

faq.question: Should I use scratch or distroless as the base image?
faq.answer: Use distroless/static for most applications. It includes CA certificates and timezone data. Use scratch only if you embed those into your binary or do not need them.

faq.question: Why set CGO_ENABLED=0?
faq.answer: Disabling CGO produces a statically linked binary that runs on scratch or distroless without libc. If you need CGO, use alpine as the runtime base instead.

faq.question: How do I cache Go module downloads in Docker?
faq.answer: Copy go.mod and go.sum first and run 'go mod download' before copying the source code. Docker will cache the module download layer as long as the dependency files do not change.
